--- a/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_IdentityComponent.tex
+++ b/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_IdentityComponent.tex
@@ -864,7 +864,17 @@ We now specialise the abstract identity-component construction to
 [The identity component of the Picard scheme]
   \label{def:pic_zero_subscheme}
   \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme}
-  
+  \uses{def:identity_component_group_scheme, def:pic_scheme,
+    def:pic_scheme_lft, thm:pic_is_group_scheme}
+  % NOTE (run-0008): the Lean body is now REAL —
+  % Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C) — because
+  % the two gate instances landed with the run-0008 FGA rewire: the group
+  % structure GrpObj (PicScheme C) is genuinely proved (Yoneda transport,
+  % thm:pic_is_group_scheme) and local finiteness is the typed-sorry
+  % carrier def:pic_scheme_lft. The former raw-sorry body is gone; all §1
+  % substrate theorems now apply to Pic^0_{C/k} definitionally. The Lean
+  % signature carries [HasPicScheme C] [PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType C].
+
   % SOURCE: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 5, opening + Prp.~5.3;
   % arXiv:math/0504020, p.~36 (read from
   % references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L2836-L2841 +
@@ -921,7 +931,8 @@ degree.
 [Degree of a Picard class]
   \label{def:divisor_degree_pic}
   \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.degree}
-  \uses{def:pic_scheme, def:hilbert_polynomial, thm:fga_pic_representability}
+  \uses{def:pic_scheme, def:hilbert_polynomial, thm:fga_pic_representability,
+    def:has_pic_scheme}
 
   % SOURCE: [Milne], ``Abelian Varieties'' (course notes, 2008),
   % \S III.1, ``Definitions and main statements'', p.~88 (PDF p.~94)
@@ -942,8 +953,10 @@ degree.
   \textit{Source: [Milne], ``Abelian Varieties'' (course notes, 2008),
   \S III.1, p.~88.}
   Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus
-  \(g\), and let \(\Pic_{C/k}\) be the Picard scheme of
-  \cref{def:pic_scheme}. The \emph{degree map}
+  \(g\) admitting a Picard scheme \(\Pic_{C/k}\) as in
+  \cref{def:pic_scheme} (run-0008: the Lean statement carries the
+  \texttt{[HasPicScheme C]} hypothesis, supplied under a \(k\)-rational
+  point via \cref{def:has_rational_point}). The \emph{degree map}
   \[
     \deg \;\colon\; \Pic_{C/k}(k) \longrightarrow \mathbb{Z}
   \]
@@ -1164,7 +1177,8 @@ automatically.
 [\(k\)-points of \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) are the kernel of the degree map]
   \label{thm:pic_zero_k_points_iff_degree_zero}
   \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme.kPoints_iff_kerDegree}
-  \uses{thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety, thm:identity_component_open_subgroup, def:pic_zero_subscheme, def:divisor_degree_pic}
+  \uses{thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety, thm:identity_component_open_subgroup, def:pic_zero_subscheme, def:divisor_degree_pic,
+    def:has_pic_scheme}
 
   % SOURCE: [Milne], ``Abelian Varieties'' (course notes, 2008),
   % \S III.1, p.~88; verbatim quote reproduced at
@@ -1174,7 +1188,10 @@ automatically.
   \S III.1, p.~88 (``\(\Pic^0(C)\)\dots\ degree zero''); the verbatim
   source quote is at \cref{def:divisor_degree_pic}.}
   Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus
-  \(g\) over a field \(k\). A \(k\)-point
+  \(g\) over a field \(k\), admitting a Picard scheme (run-0008: the Lean
+  statement carries the \texttt{[HasPicScheme C]} hypothesis, supplied
+  under a \(k\)-rational point via \cref{def:has_rational_point}). A
+  \(k\)-point
   \(\lambda \in \Pic_{C/k}(k)\) lies in the image of the inclusion of
   the identity component \(\Pic^0_{C/k} \hookrightarrow \Pic_{C/k}\)
